kwargs.agent_config_yaml:
name: basic
architecture: simple
description: Basic single-loop OpenHarness Terminal-Bench baseline agent.
model: gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview
max_turns: 30
max_tokens: 8192
tools:
- bash
- read_file
- write_file
- edit_file
- glob
- grep
components: []
definition:
  subagent_type: yaml-basic
  description: Baseline YAML coding agent for Harbor and Terminal-Bench style tasks.
  runner: yaml_workflow
  color: cyan
  permissions: []
  plan_mode_required: false
  allow_permission_prompts: false
  skills: []
  required_mcp_servers: []
  background: false
prompts:
  system: "{{ openharness_system_context }}\n\nYou are a coding agent running inside\
    \ a task environment. You have shell,\nfile, and search tools available.\n\n-\
    \ Read the task instruction carefully and inspect the filesystem before\n  editing\
    \ anything.\n- Make the smallest correct changes; verify your work with available\n\
    \  tests or task-specific checks before declaring success.\n- Use non-interactive\
    \ shell commands. Do not ask for human input.\n- Do not modify the underlying\
    \ system environment to fix application\n  bugs; fix the application code instead.\n\
    - When running commands like `wget`, avoid recursive flags that hang\n  (like\
    \ `wget -r`).\n\nStop only after the task is verified or after you have found\
    \ a concrete\nblocker that prevents further progress.\n"
  user: '{{ instruction }}

    '
subagents: {}
